title: Spicy Velvety Argentinian Pepper Stir-fry
description: A hearty and spicy hangover-curing dish that combines tender red peppers with a velvety sauce for a delicious and satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Use large, ripe ones for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Canned diced tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a low-sodium version if available.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Fresh or minced, depending on preference.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use mild olive oil for a smoother texture.

ingredients.name: Red wine vinegar (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional to add more acidity and depth of flavor.

instructions: Preheat your wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil and heat evenly.
Dice the red and green bell peppers into bite-sized pieces. Sauté them until they soften slightly, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the sautéed peppers. Cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
If using, add a few dashes of red wine vinegar to help tenderize the peppers further and enhance their flavor.
Pour in the canned tomatoes with their juices. Stir well to combine and bring the sauce to a simmer.
Season with chili flakes. Stir gently to mix evenly.
Reduce heat to low and let it cook for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until all flavors meld together and reduce slightly.
Serve hot as a side dish or alongside your favorite protein.
